label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the story again to figure out what Robin learned. The story is about Robin and his family going to a restaurant. When they're there, two homeless boys come in. The boys are dirty and smell bad, but they're smiling with blue eyes. One of them is sick. They can only afford a cup of coffee to warm up. Robin's mom sees them and orders some food for them, showing kindness without expecting anything in return. After witnessing this, Robin understands what unconditional love is, and now he saves his pocket money to help homeless people whenever he can. The question asks what Robin learned from the story. The key part is when it says, "When Robin saw what his mother did, he understood what the unconditional love was. His mother treated those boys kindly. She did not expect anything in return. Now Robin saves his own pocket money and helps homeless people whenever he can." So, the main lesson here is that Robin learned to treat others kindly without expecting anything in return. This is demonstrated by his mother's actions and his own subsequent behavior of helping homeless people with his savings. Looking at the answer choices: (A) We should treat others kindly. (B) We shouldn't laugh at others. (C) We should save pocket money. (D) We shouldn't receive others' help. The best answer is (A) We should treat others
